local_proof.description: Private demo with no real users and no real sensitive data.
local_proof.minimum: Runnable, testable, honest about missing production controls, and free of secrets or stubs.

design_partner_mvp.description: External review, pilot, competition, or enterprise design-partner engagement.
design_partner_mvp.minimum: Full baseline required unless a deferral is explicit, low risk, and user-approved.

production_ready.description: Real customers, revenue, regulated data, or operational dependency.
production_ready.minimum: Full baseline plus hardened operations, rollback, incident response, and compliance evidence.

requirements.title: Authentication and session boundary
requirements.requirement: Define how users authenticate, how sessions or tokens expire, and which endpoints or screens are public.
requirements.deferral_rule: May be deferred only for local proof with no accounts, no sensitive data, and no external network exposure.

requirements.title: Authorization and ownership checks
requirements.requirement: Every user-owned resource must enforce ownership, role, or tenant checks before read or write access.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred when user data, tenant data, or admin actions exist.

requirements.title: Schema, migrations, and constraints
requirements.requirement: Persistent data must have explicit schema, migration path, required constraints, and seed or fixture strategy.
requirements.deferral_rule: May be simplified for local proof only when using ephemeral storage with no user promise of persistence.

requirements.title: Input validation and predictable errors
requirements.requirement: All public inputs must be validated and invalid states must return predictable, non-leaky errors.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for public interfaces.

requirements.title: Primary user-flow proof
requirements.requirement: The mission-critical user flow must be traceable from objective to UI or entry point, backend/API, auth/session context, data or side effect, feedback state, and evidence.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for the core workflow; narrow scope instead.

requirements.title: System invariants and state safety
requirements.requirement: Define and enforce the state, ownership, data integrity, idempotency, side-effect, recovery, and auditability rules that must never be violated by the core workflow.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for invariants that protect user data, durable state, external side effects, or recovery.

requirements.title: Secrets and configuration hygiene
requirements.requirement: No hardcoded secrets; configuration must come from environment, managed config, or documented local examples.

requirements.title: Abuse and injection resistance
requirements.requirement: Identify likely abuse paths and guard against injection, path traversal, unsafe redirects, unsafe deserialization, and broad CORS defaults.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for externally reachable systems.

requirements.title: Critical-path test pyramid
requirements.requirement: Cover core behavior with unit tests, integration or contract tests, and at least one smoke or end-to-end path where applicable.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for core workflow behavior.

requirements.title: Logs, metrics, health, and traceability
requirements.requirement: Expose health status, structured logs, meaningful errors, and metrics or request IDs sufficient to debug the core workflow.
requirements.deferral_rule: May be reduced for local proof, but errors and logs must still be useful.

requirements.title: Deploy, rollback, and runbook path
requirements.requirement: Document how to run, deploy, smoke test, roll back, and diagnose the system.
requirements.deferral_rule: Local proof may document local run commands only; external demos need smoke and rollback notes.

requirements.title: CI/CD and observable delivery infrastructure
requirements.requirement: Define CI/CD or a local-proof substitute that runs tests, gates, dependency/security checks, build, deployment or deployability proof, environment/secrets checks, health/smoke checks, observability evidence, and rollback/runbook notes.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred for design-partner MVP, judged demos, external pilots, or any system with external network exposure.

requirements.title: Usable UI states
requirements.requirement: Frontend workflows must include loading, empty, success, validation, and error states without layout breaks.
requirements.deferral_rule: Not applicable to headless APIs; otherwise do not defer for judged demos.

requirements.title: Explicit performance budget
requirements.requirement: Define latency, page weight, data volume, and concurrency assumptions for the core workflow.
requirements.deferral_rule: May use estimates for local proof; design-partner MVP needs measured core-path evidence.

requirements.title: Dependency freshness and vulnerability posture
requirements.requirement: Dependencies must be real, current, pinned or lockfile-backed, and scanned for known vulnerabilities where tooling exists.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred when external packages are added.

requirements.title: Current dependency evidence and compatibility
requirements.requirement: High-impact dependencies must have current docs, registry, release-note, or changelog evidence; runtime and package-manager compatibility; lockfile-backed install proof; audit output; and an upgrade path.
requirements.deferral_rule: Cannot be deferred when auth, security, payments, database, AI SDK, deployment, or public-interface packages are added or upgraded.

requirements.title: Evidence-backed completion claims
requirements.requirement: Every complete claim must map to a command, artifact, screenshot, log, source, or explicit accepted deferral.
